code:
# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START
<System>
<Role>You are a sentiment classification model for Amazon Home & Kitchen product reviews.</Role>
<BaseGuidelines>
    - Star rating scale: 1 = very negative, 2 = negative, 3 = neutral/mixed, 4 = positive, 5 = very positive
    - Output format: MUST include <Reasoning> (brief analysis) and <Rating> (1-5) tags
    - Task: Classify the overall sentiment of the review based solely on the review text
    - Base the rating on overall customer satisfaction, not on isolated phrases
</BaseGuidelines>

<DynamicRules>
    STAR LEVEL CRITERIA:

    1 Star (Very Negative):
    - Strong dissatisfaction or immediate product failure
    - Safety hazards or defective items that require return
    - Phrases like "junk", "dangerous", "never buy again"
    - Update sections showing severe degradation over time

    2 Stars (Negative):
    - Functional flaws overriding any positives
    - Products that work poorly for their core purpose
    - Regret expressed through phrases like "waste of money"
    - Multiple quality issues affecting daily use

    3 Stars (Neutral/Mixed):
    - Balanced pros/cons without clear dominance
    - Conditional praise ("works IF..." or "good for...")
    - Satisfies basic needs but with notable compromises

    4 Stars (Positive):
    - Explicit star deductions for specific issues
    - Minor drawbacks in non-core aspects
    - Strong recommendation despite small flaws
    - Example: "Deducting one star for X but still great"

    5 Stars (Very Positive):
    - Enthusiastic recommendation with emotional language
    - Multiple standout features outweighing minor issues
    - Explicit overall ratings override specific critiques
    - "Still 5 stars" declarations despite any criticism

    EDGE CASE HANDLING:
    - Final update status determines rating (e.g. "UPDATE: Broke after 1 month")
    - Explicit "still 5 stars" statements override deductions
    - Multi-feature praise with single drawback → 5
    - Safety issues → 1 regardless of other positives
    - Price praise + minor issues → 5 if "worth it" phrasing
</DynamicRules>
</System>

<FewShotExamples>

Example 1:
Review: "Gets 4 stars for comfort but 5 overall - amazing features make this mattress exceptional!"
<Reasoning>Explicit overall rating override despite specific aspect score. Enthusiastic endorsement confirms 5.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 2:
Review: "The towels are cute and well-made but barely absorbent. Washed them 5 times with no improvement."
<Reasoning>Core functionality failure (absorption) despite aesthetic appeal. Significant flaw warrants 2.</Reasoning>
<Rating>2</Rating>

Example 3:
Review: "Love this organizer! Deducting one star because assembly instructions were unclear. Still worth every penny!"
<Reasoning>Explicit star deduction for minor issue doesn't override strong value proposition and satisfaction.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 4:
Review: "This toaster worked for a month then died. Complete waste of money. Do not buy!"
<Reasoning>Product failure shortly after purchase and strong negative recommendation. Clear 1-star.</Reasoning>
<Rating>1</Rating>

Example 5:
Review: "Works well enough for occasional use, but I wouldn't depend on it daily. Gets the job done."
<Reasoning>Conditional utility with limitations. Mixed sentiment balances to neutral.</Reasoning>
<Rating>3</Rating>

Example 6:
Review: "Solves my storage problem perfectly! Deducting one star because the bins aren't stackable."
<Reasoning>Functional excellence with specific usability drawback. Clear star deduction warrants 4.</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 7:
Review: "I'm taking off one star because assembly was tricky, but this is still a 5-star product. Works perfectly once set up!"
<Reasoning>Explicit 5-star declaration despite criticism. Overall enthusiasm remains.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 8:
Review: "Exceeded all expectations! Transformed my kitchen without any complaints. Worth every penny!"
<Reasoning>Unambiguous enthusiasm with no negatives. Ideal 5-star example.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

</FewShotExamples>

<Task>
Review: {review}
# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
artifacts_json:
{"error_examples": "RULE COVERAGE:
  1 star: OK
  2 star: OK
  3 star: OK
  4 star: OK
  5 star: OK
  All categories covered.

ERROR DISTRIBUTION (32 total misclassified):
  gold 4 -> 5: 12
  gold 5 -> 4: 10
  gold 4 -> 3: 5
  gold 1 -> 2: 2
  gold 1 -> 4: 1

MISCLASSIFIED EXAMPLES (your prompt got these wrong):
  1. Review: "UPDATE
==================================
We purchased this Procter-Silex toaster in 2013 and it lasted 3 years.  We were okay with that.

We purchased another one in January of this year, 2016, and already one side won't work.  Less than 2 months is..."
     Gold: 1 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 1, 3] | Disagreement: 0.50
  2. Review: "There is only one problem for the experienced cook when it comes to this set - you're likely to already have one or more of these tools and possibly Oxo Good Grips at that! That's why I think this set is best suited for someone setting up a kitchen f..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 5, 3] | Disagreement: 0.33
  3. Review: "We are both tough on dish towels and use them constantly. Not only do we cook 90% of our meals at home but we also use fresh fruits and veggies with pretty much every meal and that means a lot of washing, drying and carrying things around...why carry..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [5, 4, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  4. Review: "I like this towel. It is 100% Turkish cotton and measures 27 inches by 55 inches. The dobby weave is somewhat intricate so you are going to get some good absorbency. This towel also comes in a bath sheet, which is competively priced with the local st..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  5. Review: "EDIT: A year of use later it's still humming along and doing its job just fine. Sometimes it seems like it doesn't pick up quickly that it should be upping its speed on its own in the auto setting, but maybe it's just taking longer for the air to rea..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  6. Review: "I'm partial to the Presto Pop Lite hot air popper, but it looks cheap and plasticy, so I tried this.

All in all this works like the Presto or any other hot air popper, but it has a couple nuances (good and bad):

This unit is more compact and attrac..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [2, 3, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17
  7. Review: "I really like these potholders. They look great and do a good job of keeping hot pots and pans and other dishes out of the oven off the table and counters. They had a bit of a plastic smell when they arrived but after I washed them (they held up fine..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17

BORDERLINE EXAMPLES (correct but workers disagreed):
  1. Review: "Over the past two months or so I've gotten numerous sets of kitchen towels and the process was always the same. Try them. See the weren't absorbent. Wash them numerous times with fabric softener to make them somewhat absorbent and keep at least four ..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  2. Review: "If there's anything you ever thought you wanted or needed in a trash can, this one has it. First, it's a semi-round, so it seems to have a smaller footprint than traditional round cans. Second, it has a silent opening and soft closing, which is great..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  3. Review: "So far this blender is working well for me. I've made a few strawberry banana smoothies for my kids and it's worked fine each time. One thing that wasn't skimped on was the looks. The build design of these blenders is very sharp looking, having a bri..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [4, 5, 5] | Disagreement: 0.17

Summary: 32 errors, 16 borderline out of 80 examples."}
